methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Vatn Systems, a Series A defense and security company founded by Nelson Mills, is valued at $300M post-money on $76.5M raised to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities in the same sector and stage cohort, including Vannevar Labs, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, anchored to the current $300M post-money valuation, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $600M. The peer-median method examines 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which exhibit valuations ranging from $60M to $1.5B and a median of $1.0B. Representative peers include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI, each valued at $1.4B to $1.5B in Series B.

Triangulation across these two methods yields a valuation range of $510M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $800M. The current $300M valuation sits materially below the low end of this derived range, suggesting upside potential in a Series A extension or follow-on round. Key uncertainties include the degree to which the peer cohort's Series B valuations translate to Series A positioning and the extent of product maturation and revenue traction relative to comparables.
